title: My son was killed by police, alleges mother of missing youth in Vijayawada
content: Gade Sai Krishna, who was picked up by the police a month ago, is yet to return; family members seek judicial probe, say the police should produce him before A.P. High Court

title: Stage set for Council polls in Karnataka that could give Congress majority in Upper House
content: The Congress, which now has 37 members in the 75-member Upper House, will achieve majority with wins assured in four seats. If it manages to win all the five seats it is contesting, its tally will go to 39

title: Rebuild education system to reduce cost and stress, says Rahul in Kota
content: ‘Top five competitive exams in the country have drained the energy of the youth and financial resources of middle-class families’

title: Vellappally terms IUML a communal outfit in a secular garb
content: In a signed editorial in Yoga Nadam, Vellappally reiterates his accusation, made ahead of the Assembly election, that the IUML is a “true blue communal party wearing a secular garb”. He claimed that “divisive forces were gunning for the SNDP Yogam, and the social organisation can ill-afford to remain muted when “political parties in power cradle a communal outfit”

title: Nibe Ltd demonstrates Garudastra mortar system at Infantry School Mhow
content: The trial highlighted the system’s rapid deployment ability, high rate of fire, MRSI capability, and GPS and laser-guided munitions

title: Bangladesh PM’s adviser was stopped at Delhi airport as his name was on ‘blacklist’
content: Zahed Ur Rahman, Information & Broadcasting Adviser of Bangladesh Prime Minister Tarique Rahman was allowed by the immigration to enter India but he chose to return from the airport in Delhi, say sources

title: Two properties of key terror accused attached in J&K’s Baramulla: NIA
content: Officials say the attachment was carried out under Section 33 (1) of UA (P) Act, pursuant to the orders of NIA Special Court, Jammu

title: ED questions Veena for around 8 hours in CMRL pay-off case
content: ED is learnt to have asked her about the payments amounting to ₹2.78 crore made by CMRL to her now-defunct Exalogic IT company. The ED also raised questions on the loans worth ₹50 lakh extended to Exalogic by a firm operated by CMRL Managing Director S.N. Sasidharan Kartha, despite her company failing to make timely repayments

title: MAHE to sponsor 50 houses to be donated to meritorious students and Yakshagana artists, says Ranjan Pai
content: He lauded the gesture of the Kalaranga in getting houses constructed and donated to poor meritorious students and Yakshagana artists without any fanfare

title: Farmers warn of protests over fertilizer shortage; allege black market sales
content: SKM leaders said here that farmers are already facing shortages in Bihar and Madhya Pradesh, and fertilizer is being sold in the black market

title: Bangladesh PM’s foreign visit plans won’t cast a shadow on India-Bangladesh ties: Official sources
content: Tarique Rahman is expected to leave for his first round of foreign tours next week that will take him to Malaysia and China

title: Trump says U.S. will defend India if anyone attacks as Modi raises seafarers safety issue
content: PM Modi hoped that the peace deal with Iran would have provisions for the security of the seafarers; “As long as I am at the White House, India has a friend in the U.S.,” Mr. Trump said

title: Manipur Governor meets Shah, State’s security situation discussed
content: Ajay Kumar Bhalla is learnt to have apprised the Home Minister about the security situation in the State where tensions have escalated between the Kuki and Naga communities over the past one month.

title: Bangladesh PM’s adviser returns from Delhi airport; says he was ‘humiliated’ by immigration
content: Rahman was held for more than two hours before he was cleared to enter Delhi; by that time, he had made up his mind to return

title: Farm workers’ unions, activists announce protest from July 1 for VB-G RAM G repeal
content: NREGA Sangarsh Morcha says the new scheme will provide only 42 days of work and not 125 days as government promises; workers’ groups say the protest will continue till the law is repealed

title: Former Trinamool Minister Udayan Guha arrested in  cheating case
content: The case relates to allegations of cheating and collection of ‘cut money’ under the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana since 2016, police say

title: Old HDMC stalls razed in Hubballi following court order
content: Over 50 stalls adjoining the municipal hospital were cleared in a demolition drive led by the Municipal Commissioner

title: Three burnt to death, two injured in Chhattisgarh sand mining dispute
content: The deceased former janpad panchayat vice president Bharat Singh and his long-standing business rivals, the Tripathi family, whose members are among the accused, were both associated with the ruling BJP

title: Trinamool’s Abhishek Banerjee to meet Lok Sabha Speaker over party split
content: In a letter dated June 10 addressed to the Speaker, Mr. Banerjee urged him not to recognise or grant separate status to any rebel TMC faction in the Lok Sabha

title: Siddalinga Swami criticises Revoor for remark on Qamar-ul-Islam
content: He dares the former MLA to respond to Priyank Kharge’s repeated questions regarding the registration of the RSS

title: Telegram was warned at highest levels about app’s misuse before blanket ban: NTA D-G
content: Telegram founder had promised to make the ‘edited’ label more visible, but it was not done, says NTA Director-General Abhishek Singh, adding that the messaging app has to “adhere to norms”

title: Full body scanners on trial at four metro airports; BCAS also asks Srinagar, Jammu, Ayodhya to install them
content: Scanner trials began at Delhi, Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Kochi in May 2026; to continue for three months; airports handling over 50 lakh passengers annually asked to install scanners, which minimise the need for physical frisking

title: Delhi High Court issues notice to Centre on Telegram’s challenge to temporary ban ahead of NEET exam
content: During the hearing, Solicitor General Tushar Mehta, who represented the Centre, alleged that several channels on the platform were allegedly being used to offer leaked examination questions in exchange for money

title: After protests, three injured Kukis shifted out of Imphal hospital
content: The three youths were moved 60 km away to Churachandpur Medical College under tight security; protesters had earlier tried to storm RIMS and take away the trio who were injured in an attack on their village in Kuki-majority Kangpokpi

title: Kerala HC upholds conviction of madrassa teacher in POCSO case
content: The special court had found him guilty of all charges and had sentenced him to 20 years of imprisonment

title: Complaints filed over alleged mangrove destruction for STP near Sarovaram
content: The complaint states that there is clear evidence that trees and vegetation were uprooted using a JCB and that the area was subsequently filled with soil

title: Tomato, onion, potato prices rise within a week, yet farmers say their produce fetches very low prices
content: ‘In potato-growing States such as U.P., and Bihar too, farmers are in distress as they get as little as ₹2 or ₹3 per kilo for potatoes. But consumers are paying through their nose,’ says farmer leader Ashok Dhawale

title: E100: How India’s new flex fuel norms affect your vehicle and wallet | Business Matters
content: The government has approved rules for 100% ethanol (E100) fuel. What does this mean for motorists? From higher car costs to lower mileage, here’s a simple breakdown of flex-fuel vehicles, costs, and impact on your everyday driving.

title: Women plantation workers from Tamil Nadu joyous about free KSRTC ride
content: But Kumily depot officials say the facility could be restricted to commuters within Kerala’s borders. The average daily collection per bus used to be ₹26,000, but since the implementation of the scheme, it has dropped to below ₹8,000

title: District panchayat draws up plan to address street dog issue
content: Along with the ABC programme, the panchayat has decided to set up more shelters for dogs undergoing sterilisation and make the licensing of pet dogs mandatory

title: 1,000 home guards to be posted in Fire and Rescue Services: Chennithala
content: Action plan to be prepared for comprehensive modernisation of the force against the backdrop of climate change and resultant increase in disasters

title: Let Opposition MPs be guided by their conscience on backing NDA in Parliament: Omar Abdullah
content: We remain committed to our political ideology and will not alter our stand for the sake of power; every party is free to make its own decisions, but the NC’s position is clear, says J&K Chief Minister Omar Abdullah

title: India, Thailand review defence ties, strategic cooperation
content: Defence industry cooperation featured prominently in the talks as India and Thailand explored opportunities to deepen collaboration in defence manufacturing, research, and capability development

title: Kerala CPI to focus on educating cadre in a bid to strengthen itself organisationally
content: The CPI will launch a five-day training programme for teachers of the party school on June 20, followed by classes for its 1.5 lakh members in Kerala, and plans a people’s march to Delhi on September 1 to tackle unemployment and rising prices.

title: India’s defence production hits record ₹1.78 lakh crore in FY 2025-26
content: According to the Defence Ministry, the milestone represents a 15.6% growth over the previous fiscal year’s output of ₹1.54 lakh crore

title: SIT to move court for custody of DYFI leader in ‘kafir screenshot’ case
content: Jithin Bhaskar, a DYFI Vadakara block committee member and admin of the WhatsApp group ‘Vadakara Squad’ is suspected of sharing it with over 200 persons. However, police have not established that he is the creator of the screenshot

title: Watch: Telegram under fire: NTA’s crackdown explained | Above the Fold | 17.06.2026
content: Telegram challenges its temporary NEET block, fresh details emerge on the U.S.-Iran ceasefire deal, and Messi makes World Cup history with a hat-trick - Top stories of the day.

title: War of words erupts in U.P. as BJP ally claims split in Samajwadi Party; Akhilesh hits back
content: State Minister and SBSP president Om Prakash Rajbhar’s statement is echoed by Deputy Chief Minister Keshav Prasad Maurya, who says as many as 26 SP MPs are ready to defect

title: US Federal Reserve policy meet: Kevin Warsh-led FOMC keeps interest rates unchanged
content: Markets had broadly expected the US Federal Reserve to keep its benchmark interest rate unchanged, extending the pause that has been in place throughout the year.

title: Dell CEO Michael Dell makes one of largest public university donations in US history
content: Michael Dell has gifted a staggering $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin, propelling the creation of a groundbreaking AI-native hospital and research campus. This substantial donation, adding to his family's legacy of support, will also bolster scholarships, advanced computing, and vital medical research, underscoring Dell's enduring connection to his alma mater.

title: Economics of buying versus renting a home: What makes sense?
content: India's dream of homeownership faces a reality check in 2026 as property prices and rents surge. Millions of urban Indians are now weighing the financial implications of buying versus renting. While buying offers long-term security, renting might be the smarter move for many amidst rising costs and shrinking affordable housing supply.

title: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff shares selfie with PM Modi at G7 summit: What he said
content: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff shared a selfie with Prime Minister Narendra Modi at the G7 Summit in France, discussing AI's role in digital governance. Benioff also met other tech leaders like Google DeepMind's CEO, focusing on responsible AI deployment and online safety for minors. PM Modi engaged with various global leaders on diverse issues.

title: Sooryavanshi turns eagle to grab a stunner during IND A vs AFG A - Watch
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi stole the show with a sensational diving catch to dismiss Khalid Taniwal during India A's Tri-Nation Series clash against Afghanistan A. The 15-year-old's athletic effort helped India A tighten their grip on the match, which they eventually won by 101 runs to seal a place in the final, with Nishant Sindhu starring with four wickets.

title: Shah directs enhancement of national cyber crime helpline '1930'
content: Home Minister Amit Shah has ordered a significant upgrade of the national cyber crime helpline '1930'. The directive emphasizes integrating advanced technology like AI and multilingual support to enhance reporting and redressal. Shah stressed swift action on cyber financial fraud complaints, including prioritizing bank account freezing issues, and called for stronger measures against mule accounts.

title: 3 months after marriage, UP woman found dead; family accuses in-laws of murder
content: A newlywed woman died under suspicious circumstances in Etawah, three months after her marriage. Her family alleges murder by her in-laws, claiming she was strangled and then hanged. Police have taken the father-in-law into custody, while the husband and mother-in-law are at large and being sought.

title: Peter Thiel's secret Dialog society 'exposed' in data leak: Who's on the list
content: A data leak has exposed members of Dialog, the secretive invitation-only society co-founded by Peter Thiel in 2006. First surfaced by hacktivist maia arson crimew, the 222-name list for its 2026 retreat includes NATO's top Europe commander, US senators, Treasury secretary Scott Bessent, Elon Musk and Stanford president Jonathan Levin. The records reveal off-the-record sessions on WWIII and cult-building, a members' dating app, and leaked political-leaning data.

title: 2 seats, 3 candidates: Can INDIA bloc hold its flock together for Rajya Sabha polls in Jharkhand?
content: The upcoming Rajya Sabha elections in Jharkhand are shaping up to be a nail-biter. The INDIA bloc, boasting 56 MLAs, appears to have the upper hand with a potential for two seats, but the BJP, rallying behind an independent candidate, is poised to sow discord through cross-voting, reminiscent of previous electoral surprises in Bihar and Odisha.

title: 'Finish him today': Wife stabs husband to death after being caught talking to boyfriend on phone
content: A Kanpur woman allegedly stabbed her husband to death after a dispute over her phone call with her boyfriend. The husband confronted her, and during the argument, the boyfriend reportedly told her to "Finish your husband." The woman attacked him multiple times with a knife, leading to his death 11 days later in the hospital.

title: Taylor Swift’s heartbreaks with Joe Alwyn and Matty Healy ultimately led her to Travis Kelce
content: Taylor Swift’s 2023 saw rapid romantic relationship with Matty Healy following her painful Joe Alwyn breakup. It was the Eras Tour that provided her solace. Her album The Tortured Poets Department reportedly draws parallels to her Healy romance. While The Life of a Showgirl celebrates her current happily ever after with Kansas City Chiefs tight end Travis Kelce. She calls him her happiness.

title: Crissy Froyd claims Mike Silver liked old bikini photos after Russini Story Row
content: Crissy Froyd has accused The Athletic's Mike Silver of liking old bikini photos on her Instagram immediately after she published a column about the Mike Vrabel-Dianna Russini scandal, alleging the behaviour was targeted. Froyd, who was previously fired from USA Today Sports over her criticism of Russini, has alleged that at least six female reporters admitted to her that they traded intimacy for NFL access. Meanwhile, Russini has resigned from The Athletic, which is now conducting an internal investigation.

title: New claim about Jorge Messi’s health reframes Lionel Messi’s World Cup Tears
content: Lionel Messi revealed that his emotional reaction after Argentina’s World Cup win was unrelated to football. According to comments made by journalist Eduardo Feinmann on Radio Mitre, concerns about the health of Messi’s father, Jorge Messi, may have been behind the tears. Despite the personal situation, Messi starred in Argentina’s 3-0 victory over Algeria.

title: Mandhana becomes first-ever batter across men's and women's T20Is to...
content: Smriti Mandhana scripted history by becoming the first player across men's and women's T20Is to hit 600 fours. The India vice-captain struck 74 off 47 balls and, alongside Shafali Verma (55), added 115 runs for the opening wicket against the Netherlands. Late cameos from Richa Ghosh and Deepti Sharma helped India post a record Women's T20 World Cup total of 209/5.

title: New resource race: G7 joins forces to challenge China’s critical minerals dominance
content: G7 leaders are uniting to lessen reliance on China for vital minerals, essential for defense and green tech. Plans include coordinated stockpiling, enhanced recycling, and a new platform with the IEA. The goal is to significantly cut dependence on single suppliers by 2030, fostering more secure global supply chains.

title: Boss baby: Trump says 'I’m the boss' as critics say he’s getting bossed by Iran
content: President Trump, facing criticism for a potential Iran deal, declared himself "the boss" while threatening renewed military action. Critics argue the proposed Memorandum of Understanding offers Iran significant concessions, including sanctions relief and asset unfreezing, in exchange for vague nuclear assurances. This has sparked domestic debate and bipartisan opposition in Congress.

title: Minor girl forced into illegal conversion and marriage; Kutch police arrest Ajmer Dargah Imam
content: Rapar police have arrested an Imam from Ajmer for allegedly facilitating the illegal religious conversion and Nikah of a 17-year-old girl. The girl's family reported her abduction by Jahin Abdul Chauhan, who allegedly assaulted her and forced her into marriage after conversion. Both accused are in police custody.

title: Govt raises ceiling prices of BCG, Measles vaccines by 21%
content: The government has increased ceiling prices for BCG, Measles, and Measles-Rubella vaccines by approximately 21%. This decision follows a review after the Serum Institute of India appealed an earlier pricing order. The revision aims to address the manufacturer's concerns and ensure continued availability of these vital public health vaccines easily.

title: 'It's a rough profession': Trump on death of 3 Indian seafarers during meeting with PM Modi
content: Prime Minister Modi and US President Trump met at the G7 Summit, prioritizing maritime security and the safety of Indian seafarers. Modi stressed the vital importance of open shipping routes, particularly the Strait of Hormuz, and commended Trump's efforts in West Asia. The leaders also discussed trade deals, signaling renewed energy in India-US relations after a period of strain.

title: Madras HC stays church project near temple, flags possible bad intentions
content: The court said it would also take on record the submission of the petitioner that “following change of govt headed by chief minister C Joseph Vijay posters have appeared in some parts of Tamil Nadu calling for construction of churches in every village.”

title: Age of software export controls starts with a bug report as US treats AI model like a missile
content: The United States has expanded export controls to cover advanced AI models. This means access to powerful AI is now treated like a physical export. Companies like Anthropic must restrict access for foreign nationals. This action signifies a major shift in how technology is regulated. It suggests AI is now considered strategic infrastructure, not just software.

title: Worrying sight for India: Star spinner stretchered off after ankle injury
content: The incident took place in the sixth over of the Netherlands innings. Shreyanka had bowled just one delivery when she hurt herself while trying to stop the ball in the field.

title: Rakesh Bedi addresses claims of Dhurandhar script coming from PMO
content: Rakesh Bedi discussed his role in 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' and his creative inputs, particularly regarding humor. He revealed that a memorable dialogue about "white buttocks" was a spontaneous creation during filming, which director Aditya Dhar loved and incorporated digitally.

title: Longer than World War I: 10 ways Russia-Ukraine conflict has changed the world for decades to come
content: The Russia-Ukraine war, now longer than WWI, is fundamentally reshaping global order. It has triggered NATO expansion, a European arms race, and elevated drone warfare. Russia faces deep dependency on China, while North Korea and Iran profit as key arms suppliers. Beijing learns from Moscow's failures, and India navigates US pressure over Russian oil.

title: Video of namaz being offered inside Gujarat hospital sparks row
content: A video showing individuals praying inside Vadodara's SSG Hospital OPD has sparked a controversy. Hospital administration is investigating the incident, which reportedly occurred Monday evening. Authorities confirmed the individuals appeared to be patient relatives, and their prayers caused no disruption to services. The VHP and Bajrang Dal have sought clarification from the hospital.

title: Fuel, forex and conflict hit airlines: Domestic carriers’ profits may fall 15% this year
content: Indian airlines anticipate a 10-15% drop in operating profits this fiscal year. Soaring aviation fuel prices, airspace limitations, and a weakening rupee, exacerbated by the Middle East conflict, are significantly impacting the sector. Despite potential fuel price moderation, higher costs and lease rentals will continue to pressure airline finances.

title: India-UK FTA to take effect from July 15, UK envoy calls it a 'historic moment'
content: India and the UK will implement their landmark free trade agreement on July 15, significantly reducing tariffs on key products like whisky and automobiles. This historic pact is set to boost bilateral trade by billions and enhance economic cooperation, with businesses having 28 days to prepare for its benefits.

title: NFL trade rumors: Bengals, Giants Eye $3M Cardinals Star
content: The Arizona Cardinals’ star player James Conner is being speculated to be traded from the team ahead of the new season, sparking chaos among fans about his future. Though many teams have been linked to him, the Cincinnati Bengals and the New York Giants have emerged as potential landing spots for James Conner.

title: Shreyas Iyer surpasses Virat Kohli, becomes joint second-fastest Indian to...
content: Shreyas Iyer achieved a significant milestone in his ODI career. He became the joint second-fastest Indian batter to reach 3,000 runs. Iyer reached this landmark in his 72nd innings. This places him alongside Shikhar Dhawan and ahead of Virat Kohli. The achievement occurred during India's second ODI against Afghanistan in Lucknow. Shubman Gill remains the fastest Indian to this mark.

title: From Iran missions to California fireball: What we know about fatal B-52 crash
content: A US Air Force B-52H Stratofortress crashed shortly after takeoff at Edwards Air Force Base, killing all eight personnel aboard. The iconic bomber, a symbol of American air power, was on a routine test mission. Aerial footage showed the aircraft bursting into flames, with tracking data indicating a rapid, uncontrolled descent.

title: Pentagon renaming of Pacific command deepens unease as Trump assures 'we are there for India'
content: The Pentagon has reverted the Indo-Pacific Command to its former name, Pacific Command, sparking concerns about India's strategic standing. While officials claim no mission change, analysts interpret the move, coinciding with a less warm Trump-Modi meeting, as a potential recalibration of US Asia policy and diminished enthusiasm for the Quad alliance.

title: 'We are very close to deal': Trump signals progress on India-US trade agreement
content: US President Donald Trump indicated significant progress in trade talks with India, stating a deal is "very close." This follows intensified negotiations and an upcoming visit by the US Trade Representative to finalize a bilateral trade agreement. Most issues are reportedly settled, with discussions focusing on minor details for the initial phase, expected before July 24.

title: Zeenat Aman reveals her Hindu name and why she chose not to follow religion
content: Veteran actress Zeenat Aman revealed her birth name, Laliteshwari, and discussed her upbringing with a Hindu mother and Muslim father. She explained that her diverse cultural exposure led her to embrace humanity, kindness, and love as her religion, rather than adhering to any specific faith.

title: Gill turns up the heat, leaves Virender Sehwag behind to become ...
content: Gill's innings came after India found themselves at 96 for 2. He then joined forces with Ishan Kishan for a 224-run partnership that put India in control of the contest. Both batters went on to score centuries.

title: Prince Yadav's dream debut turns sour as maiden ODI wicket ruled no-ball
content: Debut pacer Prince Yadav experienced a moment of heartbreak as his maiden ODI wicket was disallowed due to a marginal no-ball. Despite the unfortunate turn, Yadav's delivery troubled Afghanistan opener Rahmanullah Gurbaz. This incident occurred after India posted a formidable 402, powered by Shubman Gill's 154 and Ishan Kishan's 125.

title: Now, rebellion in SP? UP minister Rajbhar makes big claim, Akhilesh Yadav responds
content: Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav has dismissed claims of a major split within his party. He accused the Bharatiya Janata Party of a history of engineering defections in opposition parties. Yadav stated the Samajwadi Party is prepared for such challenges. These remarks come amid political positioning for the 2027 Uttar Pradesh assembly elections.

title: Illegal Lion Safari Racket Busted In Gujarat, 7 Arrested, Thar Seized
content: The Forest Department has busted a major unauthorised wildlife viewing racket operating in Gujarat's Gir Somnath district.

title: Rape Case Filed Against Matrimonial Fraud Accused In Gujarat
content: The second case against Karim includes serious legal provisions for rape, creation of forged or counterfeit documents and extortion.

title: Team Thackeray Rebels Write To Speaker, Announcement Likely Soon: Sources
content: The Thackeray camp has called for a party meet at 11 am tomorrow and issued a notice to the rebel MPs, declaring that action will be taken against them if they fail to turn up.

title: Open Hormuz, $300-Billion Fund, No Nukes: US Officials Release Iran Deal Text
content: Hormuz reopening, $300-billion reconstruction fund, no nuclear weapons: US officials release text of Iran deal

title: India, UK Set July 15 For Historic Free Trade Pact's Rollout
content: The announcement was made jointly by the two governments after completing all domestic ratification and implementation procedures, setting the stage for a significant expansion in bilateral trade, investment and services exports.

title: Akal Takht Row Escalates: Sukhbir Badal Attacks Mann, BJP Seeks CBI Probe
content: Shiromani Akali Dal leader Sukhbir Singh Badal has launched a scathing attack on Mann, accusing him of challenging the authority of the Akal Takht Sahib - the highest temporal authority for Sikhs.

title: How 'City Of Lakes' Bhopal Became Repeated Target for Terror Recruiters
content: Mohammad Faraz, 35, has been arrested and was allegedly being groomed to become a "lone wolf" attacker.

title: Somalian Pirates Step Up Attacks As Ships Reroute Amid Middle East War
content: As commercial passenger flights begin from Noida International Airport (Jewar) from tomorrow, India's aviation sector will add another operational airport to its growing network.

title: US-Iran LIVE Updates: Trump Says Iran Accord To Be Signed 'Shortly', 'Maybe' Thursday Or Friday
content: US-Iran War LIVE Updates: Netanyahu pledged that Iran would never be allowed to acquire nuclear weapons, regardless of the terms of any agreement.

title: "Tomorrow, Maybe Next Day": Trump Injects Uncertainty Into Iran Deal Signing
content: The document, titled the "Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding between the United States of America and the Islamic Republic of Iran," was eventually released by Washington on Wednesday.

title: Why India Has A Seat At The G7 Table Without Being A Member
content: India is not a formal member of the G7, which includes the US, UK, Canada, France, Germany, Italy and Japan. Yet, it is regularly invited as a special guest, showing how much importance the group now gives to New Delhi's voice.

title: For Netanyahu, A "Softer Touch" Message From Trump On Lebanon
content: US President Donald Trump said on Wednesday that Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu could use a "softer touch" in Lebanon in comments made at the close of a G7 summit in France.

title: PM Modi Pushes Human-Centric AI At G7 Summit, Flags Deepfake Risks
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Wednesday urged world leaders to place human values at the centre of Artificial Intelligence (AI) development, calling for global cooperation to ensure the technology is deployed safely.

title: Analysis | After Opposition Rebellions, NDA May Need Just 6 More Votes For Delimitation
content: In April, a Constitution amendment bill linked to delimitation, that was part of the women's reservation package, was blocked by the combined weight of the Opposition. The bill was stopped cold by 54 votes in the Lok Sabha. Were the bill to be presen

title: Pakistani Currency Notes Found During Search In J&K's Poonch, Probe Launched
content: The security forces also recovered a paper slip mentioning several suspected Pakistani mobile numbers in the village.

title: How Trump's Iran Deal Is Creating Trouble For Netanyahu
content: While Washington appears focused on reducing tensions and moving towards a settlement, Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu has maintained that Israel's operations are far from over which has created a growing gap between the two allies.

title: G7 Summit 2026 LIVE Updates: PM Modi Meet Zelensky On Sidelines
content: G7 Summit 2026 LIVE Updates: This is India's 13th participation in the G7 as a guest nation and PM Modi's seventh consecutive appearance at the summit.

title: Explained: Why India, Pak Never Target Each Other's Nuclear Installations
content: This restraint is not accidental. It is rooted in a formal bilateral agreement that stands as one of the most durable confidence-building measures in the fragile South Asian region.
